content: Hey, I'm feeling really sluggish after moving to Pittsburgh from the Bay Area. I used to hike all the time, but now I'm stuck inside working from home as a Fashion Buyer.
That can be tough! What kind of exercises are you doing now to stay active?
To be honest, not much. I've been trying to get into speedcubing as a way to relax, but it's not exactly a workout. I do enjoy playing tennis on the weekends, though.
Speedcubing is a great hobby! And tennis is excellent exercise. Have you considered joining a local tennis club or gym to stay active during the week?
That's not a bad idea. I've been meaning to check out the tennis courts at Frick Park. Maybe I can find a league to join. My friend, Alex, plays there and says it's a lot of fun.
Frick Park is beautiful! You should definitely check it out. What's your current fitness goal? Are you trying to lose weight or just stay healthy?
I'd like to lose a few pounds, but mostly I just want to feel more energetic. I've been having trouble sleeping since the move, and I think exercise could help.
Sleep is so important! Exercise can definitely help with that. Have you tried establishing a bedtime routine or creating a sleep-conducive environment?

Not really. I've been meaning to get some blackout curtains for my bedroom, but I haven't had a chance yet. I've also been trying to get into drone photography, so I've been staying up late editing photos.
Drone photography is a great hobby! It's understandable that you'd want to spend time on it. Maybe try setting a specific time each day to work on your photos, so you can wind down before bed.
That's a good idea. I'll try to do that. I've also been thinking about starting a new origami project to help me relax before bed.
Origami is a great way to unwind! What kind of origami do you like to make?
I've been making a lot of cranes and boxes. I find it really soothing. Maybe I'll start making some origami animals to decorate my home office.
That sounds like a great idea! Having a creative outlet can be really helpful for stress relief. Do you have a dedicated space for your origami and drone photography?

Yeah, I have a little desk in my home office where I work on my projects. It's got a great view of the city, which is nice. I've been thinking about entering a local DJing competition, too, so I've been practicing my sets.
That's exciting! DJing is a great way to express yourself creatively. What kind of music do you like to play?
I'm really into electronic music right now. I've been listening to a lot of techno and trance. It's great to work out to, too.
Electronic music is excellent for working out! Maybe you can create a playlist for your tennis matches or workouts. What's your favorite thing to do on a Sunday morning?
I love playing tennis on Sunday mornings. It's a great way to start the day. Afterward, I usually grab brunch with my friends at this great cafe in Shadyside.
That sounds like a perfect Sunday morning! Do you have any fun plans for the upcoming weekend?
Actually, I'm thinking about going to the Pittsburgh Marathon on May 1st to cheer on my friend, Rachel. She's been training for months, and I'm really proud of her.
That's great! The Pittsburgh Marathon is a big event. I'm sure your friend will appreciate your support. You should be proud of her for all her hard work!
Definitely. I'm planning on making her a little origami crane to give her good luck. I think it'll be a nice touch.
Aww, that's so sweet! I'm sure she'll love it. Good luck to your friend, and I hope you have a great time cheering her on!
Thanks! I'm looking forward to it. I think I've got a good plan in place to stay active and healthy now. Thanks for the chat!
You're welcome, Morgan! It was great chatting with you. Feel free to reach out if you need any more advice or just want to talk about your hobbies.
